# Synthesized Context
<!-- generated by gsd-doc-synthesizer — do not hand-edit -->

---

## Topic: Project positioning and business framing

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md

The EDGAR/COT system is a worked example demonstrating an AI governance/evaluation methodology, not a product in itself. The intended audience includes AI PM / product owner / business-data hiring contexts. The project is positioned around the gap between a working demo and a system the business can run unsupervised.

Core reframe: "Autonomy is an evaluation problem, not an automation problem."

The differentiated contribution is:
1. Domain-specific ground truth (XBRL facts + accounting identities) as calibration substrate rather than hand labels or LLM self-confidence
2. Honest confidence derivation from provenance + cross-check (not self-report)
3. Multi-source integrity model spanning EDGAR extraction and COT data under one risk-tiered layer

---

## Topic: Regulatory alignment

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md

US — SR 11-7 (Federal Reserve / OCC Model Risk Management):
- Sound model validation → §4.1 validation layer + §4.4 metrics
- Ongoing monitoring → §4.5 drift detection
- Governance/policies/controls/effective challenge → §4.2 routing + §4.3 always-escalate triggers

Singapore — MAS:
- FEAT Principles (2018), Veritas Initiative (2020–2023), Project MindForge (2023–)
- Proposed Guidelines on AI Risk Management: consultation closed 31 Jan 2026, expected finalised 2026 with 12-month transition. Cover all FIs, explicitly include Generative AI and AI agents, risk-based/proportionate.
- Status as of mid-2026: proposed, not yet in force — framing as "ahead of the curve" is accurate.
- Mapping: risk materiality assessment → §4.2; AI life-cycle controls → §4.1/§3/§4.2–4.3; meaningful human oversight → §4.3/§4.4; pre-deployment review + post-deployment monitoring → §6/§4.5; documentation/auditability → provenance per field + decision log.

EU AI Act: August 2026 human-oversight requirements make "demonstrable human oversight of automated decisions" a compliance requirement for enterprises. Project can be positioned as that governance capability.

---

## Topic: Open questions requiring resolution before build

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md

1. Does the reader output structured extractions, summaries/Q&A, or both? (Determines §4.1 validator shape — field validation vs. claim-against-source-span validation.)
2. What downstream actions can an extraction trigger? (Defines the highest-stakes always-escalate cases.)
3. Which form types are in scope first? (10-K/10-Q lean heavily on XBRL; 8-K is narrative-heavy and needs more hand-labeling.)
4. Is a human reviewer available for the sampled queue, or will the builder act as reviewer initially?

---

## Topic: Prior art and positioning context

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md

The eval/HITL framework is an established LLMOps pattern (Galileo, Weights & Biases, Patronus, practitioner writeups through 2025–26): confidence-band routing, deterministic always-escalate rules, sampled post-hoc review (~5–10% of auto-decisions), feedback into scorer calibration. The project is not architecturally novel; the contribution is synthesis, EDGAR-specific ground truth leverage, and the honest confidence derivation.

The canonical failure mode in the literature — confident, well-formatted agent decision based on stale/misread data — is the one this design explicitly addresses via XBRL cross-validation replacing LLM self-reported confidence.

---

## Topic: Honest gap / limitations

source: C:/RAG_workbench/docs/specs/eval-layer-spec.md

The project is a personal PoC with no real users or ROI numbers. PM/owner interviews press on business impact. Recommended mitigations:
1. Run one shadow deployment slice and cite a real result (threshold X → false-escalation Y%, agreement Z%)
2. If not feasible, frame deliverable explicitly as a reusable governance methodology
